For years Camden, N.J., ranked among the most dangerous U.S. cities. In 2012, it experienced its deadliest year on record.
That year, the city averaged more than one murder per week. In the summer, killings happened on average twice weekly.
“The relationships, the trust, the confidence in police just wasn’t there.”
Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
After Camden’s police force was shuttered in 2013, the state created a new county force that was community oriented.
“The mind-set back then was hit them as hard as you can. Now, it’s make sure you stop by and take a case of water to Mrs. Jones.”
Gabriel Rodriguez, Camden County police chief
Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
Progress in Camden means violence has fallen profoundly. As residents work to fortify the reduction, they’re also celebrating what has flourished in its absence.
“We are comfortable enough to walk our kids two, three blocks down the street.”
